title: Moog Technology Keeps Artemis II Astronauts Safe During Historic Lunar Mission
summary: Moog Inc. provided critical technology for the Artemis II mission, ensuring the safety of astronauts during their historic lunar journey. The company specializes in precision motion and fluid control systems.

title: Orion splashes down to successfully end Artemis 2 mission
summary: The Artemis 2 mission, the first human mission beyond Earth orbit in over 50 years, successfully concluded with the Orion spacecraft splashing down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10. The mission marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: HawkEye 360 files to go public
summary: HawkEye 360, a company specializing in space-based radio-frequency signals intelligence, has filed to go public. The Virginia-based firm serves government agencies with its satellite capabilities.

title: Spacety raises $190 million to scale satellite manufacturing, plans IPO
summary: Spacety, a Chinese satellite manufacturer, secured $190 million in funding to expand its satellite production and data services. The company also announced plans for an initial public offering (IPO).

title: GSOA and Novaspace Launch the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026 in Singapore
summary: GSOA and Novaspace are launching the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026, a new flagship event scheduled for May 19, 2026, at The Fullerton Hotel in Singapore.

title: Commerce Department budget proposal revives concerns about TraCSS
summary: A budget proposal for the Department of Commerce has raised concerns about the future of the civil space traffic management system (TraCSS) currently under development. This has sparked renewed doubts about the program's viability.

title: China’s Chang’e-7 arrives at spaceport for lunar south pole exploration mission
summary: China's Chang'e-7 lunar spacecraft has arrived at Wenchang spaceport for launch preparations. The mission, targeting the lunar south pole, is planned for liftoff in the second half of 2026.

title: NASA prepares for Artemis 2 return
summary: NASA is preparing for the Artemis 2 mission's return, with a final test involving reentry on April 10 followed by a splashdown off the California coast.

title: PLD Space secures $35 million European Investment Bank loan for MIURA 5 launcher
summary: PLD Space secured a $35 million loan from the European Investment Bank to fund the final development of its MIURA 5 lightweight rocket. The MIURA 5 is designed to deploy payloads of up to 1 ton to low Earth orbit.

title: Heather Pringle on the upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of a major space organization, discusses upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space on the Space Minds podcast. The interview highlights her insights as a retired US Air Force Major General.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the flight of Artemis 2. Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik provide insights into the mission.

title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts (video)
summary: SpaceX is set to launch Northrop Grumman's second 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to the International Space Station (ISS) on April 11. The launch will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
summary: NASA is not resting after the successful Artemis 2 moon mission and has ambitious plans for future lunar exploration. The agency is focusing on Artemis 3 and beyond to further its goals for Earth's nearest neighbor.

title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts successfully returned to Earth on April 10, completing a historic mission around the moon. The mission set records and paved the way for future lunar exploration.

title: NEAF 2026 returns to New York this weekend with all-star astronaut lineup and 'world's largest' space expo
summary: NEAF 2026, the world's largest space expo, returns to New York with an all-star astronaut lineup. The event will gather thousands of enthusiasts, professionals, and skywatchers.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts witnessed a rare solar eclipse from beyond the Moon, with NASA releasing a timelapse showing the Moon crossing the Sun. This provided a unique extended view of totality and the solar corona.

title: Why I'm hunting for Comet Pan-STARRS right now — before it's too late
summary: A rare long-period comet, C/2025 R3 (Pan-STARRS), will be visible before sunrise in April after 170,000 years. Stargazers are advised on key dates and viewing tips to spot the comet.

title: 'They’re not shipping cows up to Mars': 'For All Mankind' creators talk scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5 (interview)
summary: The creators of the TV show 'For All Mankind' discuss the scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5, emphasizing that the series does not depict unrealistic scenarios like shipping cows to Mars.

title: NASA took this camera gear to space aboard Artemis 2, and you can own it, too!
summary: NASA is auctioning off camera gear used aboard the Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years. The cameras, which captured stunning space imagery, are now available for public purchase.

title: Artemis 2 spies a crescent Earth above the moon's horizon | Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026
summary: A new image from the Artemis 2 mission shows a crescent Earth above the moon's horizon. The photo was featured as the Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent CRS NG-23 Cygnus mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing preparations at the company's facility in Texas.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a record by conducting a lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches coincide with the midpoint of NASA's Artemis II mission.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission carrying the first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a major milestone 50 years after Apollo as it heads toward the Moon for the Artemis II mission.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission marks humanity's return to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This follows the historic Apollo 17 mission, which concluded in 1972.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the news without additional context provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency’s technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved accuracy or efficiency in directional control systems. It highlights innovations that could enhance mission performance in the space industry.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The experiment showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials efficiently in microgravity, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functionalities, potentially revolutionizing space-based computing. This advancement could enhance satellite and spacecraft capabilities.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team received confirmation that the spacecraft is operational.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events for industry to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025. These events will outline planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in ESA's Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo announced a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. The company aims to enhance power capabilities for its upcoming flight.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog is supplying over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. The company's contributions span the entire flight system.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: Artemis II's Orion spacecraft features the European Service Module (ESM), built by Airbus Defence and Space. This collaboration between Airbus and ESA could boost European spaceflight ambitions.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has closed a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with participation from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch discusses the impact of deadlines and capability gates on competition in the space industry, highlighting how constraints can influence winners and losers in launch capabilities.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. The announcement highlights the company's focus on advancing its satellite technology.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. The piece highlights the expanding scope and challenges of ISAM as a key trend in the space industry.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-made radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. The technology serves as an early warning system for space radiation threats.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: Advocates and lawmakers are preparing to oppose the Trump administration's proposed $18.8 billion NASA budget, which includes significant cuts to science and STEM missions. This marks a recurring conflict over NASA funding priorities.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with co-leads Activate Capital and Shield Capital. This investment will support the company's growth in the space industry.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its adoption of Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi across its entire fleet in November 2023. A company executive explained the airline's decision to switch to Starlink's connectivity services.

title: Amazon CEO Targets Mid-2026 Launch for Amazon Leo Service
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that Amazon's satellite constellation service, Amazon Leo, is scheduled to launch in mid-2026. The exact launch date was not specified in the letter to shareholders.

title: BryceTech Report Shows SpaceX Accounted for 50% of Launches in 2025
summary: A BryceTech report reveals that SpaceX accounted for 50% of all launches in 2025, highlighting the company's dominant position in the space industry. The report also notes a growing trend toward small satellite deployments.

title: EnduroSat and Shield Space Strike European Partnership for Defense Missions
summary: EnduroSat and Shield Space have formed a strategic partnership to jointly operate space defense missions in Europe. The collaboration aims to leverage their combined technologies for on-orbit operations.

title: Network Integration Remains a Challenge in Pushing Data to the Tactical Edge
summary: Commercial space leaders are working to improve network integration to enable faster data processing at the tactical edge for decision-making. This is crucial for meeting the speed requirements of modern conflicts.

title: Vantor Introduces New Satellite Classes for High Resolution Imaging, Frequent Revisits
summary: Vantor launched two new classes of electro-optical imaging satellites designed for high-resolution imagery and frequent revisits. The innovation aims to enhance commercial satellite operations and spatial intelligence services.

title: Space Force Awards 14 Companies Space Domain Awareness Contracts Under “Andromeda”
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 14 companies over $1.8 billion in contracts for space domain awareness under the 'Andromeda' program. The contracts, managed by Space Systems Command, aim to enhance reconnaissance capabilities in geosynchronous orbit.

title: NSG Names Haithem Alfaraj as its Second CEO
summary: Neo Space Group (NSG) appointed Haithem Mohammed Mojil Alfaraj as its second CEO, replacing Martijn Blanken who led the company since its inception.

title: In-Space Maneuverability Startup Portal Raises Series A as it Shifts into Production Mode
summary: Portal Space Systems raised $50 million in a Series A funding round to advance its maneuverable spacecraft technology and expand production. The startup is shifting into production mode while growing its team and manufacturing capacity.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. Eiichi Yonekura, the company's President & CEO, discussed these plans in a live interview.
